picks.narrative: IBP makes PIT 56.7% fair versus a 45.8% no-vig market price — a 10.4pp gap. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): CLE's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +116 still sits inside the playable range (to -111). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

negative_drivers.detail: CLE's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road.

negative_drivers.detail: CLE defense grades +22 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind.

picks.narrative: The model sees WSH as a 60.2% side; the market is pricing 50.2%, leaving a 10.2pp edge. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.9pp), defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is home field (~-0.7pp): OAK's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +100 still sits inside the playable range (to -128). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

picks.narrative: IBP values COL 8.5pp higher than the market — 60.7% model vs 51.6% implied. Several smaller signals stack the same way — home field (~+1.1pp), pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.8pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.5pp): CIN defense grades +15 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -109 still sits inside the playable range (to -131). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.

picks.narrative: The model prices SF at 42.7% to win where the no-vig market implies 36.2%, a 6.8pp disagreement. There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): SEA's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +178 still sits inside the playable range (to +158). This is mostly a price play — the model likes the number more than the matchup, so the baseball case is mixed.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on STL by 6.6pp — 57.1% fair against a 50.5% price. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.7pp): Michael McGreevy 4.24 xFIP vs Merrill Kelly 6.09 xFIP. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): ARI defense grades +16 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -102 still sits inside the playable range (to -113). The path is exploiting the ARI weakness the model flags before they can stabilize.
